adapter_policy.duplicate_policy: preserve every native record and report tool-specific record, unique-record, and duplicate-record totals
adapter_policy.canonicalization_policy: lowercase and whitespace-normalize instruction text; preserve native sequence and source line; canonicalize addresses as 16-digit hexadecimal strings

normalized_relations.reason: ROPgadget, Ropper, and ropr expose decoder-backed gadget records rather than a program-header-authoritative raw executable-byte observation. No baseline-native total is substituted for this relation.

normalized_relations.comparison_rule: Retain and label each baseline's own return-terminated records. Tool-native totals are not cross-tool gadget populations.
Compare only an exact two-instruction pop-rdi-return relation emitted by the named baseline. This does not become an x64lens semantic-exact or decoder-backed population.
True when the baseline native output contains at least one canonical_exact_pop_rdi_ret relation.

tasks.reason: The public CLI has no report-suppressed scanner-only path. Text or JSON report timing is not a truthful substitute for isolated scanner cost.
tasks.decision_owner: diagnostic evidence and a reviewed instrumentation seam

tasks.work_scope_note: Runs the complete current analysis pipeline and JSON adapter.
Current JSON output shares the same report body and analysis facts as gadgets; the command identity differs, so this is not yet an independently broader workload.

baselines.work_scope_note: ROPgadget performs Capstone-backed ROP discovery over its own executable-segment and canonicalization rules, restricted to pop/ret mnemonics, five-byte search depth, and ROP-only output.
baselines.task_equivalence_note: Only the canonical exact pop-rdi-return relation and derived binary fact are normalized across tools. Runtime and native totals remain qualified by this tool-specific work scope.

baselines.work_scope_note: Ropper performs Capstone-backed ROP discovery under its own section, quality, duplicate, cache, and instruction-count rules with color disabled; --single disables its multi-process scanning path for this diagnostic condition.
baselines.task_equivalence_note: Only the canonical exact pop-rdi-return relation and derived binary fact are normalized across tools. Runtime and native totals remain qualified by this tool-specific work scope.

baselines.work_scope_note: ropr performs its native multithreaded x86 ROP discovery under its own executable-range, quality, duplicate, and maximum-instruction rules with JOP and syscall records disabled.
baselines.task_equivalence_note: Only the canonical exact pop-rdi-return relation and derived binary fact are normalized across tools. Runtime and native totals remain qualified by this tool-specific work scope.

claim_boundaries: Diagnostic rows and normalized artifacts are mutable development evidence only.
A command label does not prove a distinct amount of analysis work.
No generic gadget_count is comparable before candidate-definition reconciliation.
Baseline-native totals remain tool-specific and are never substituted for x64lens raw, exact, semantic-exact, unknown, decoder-backed, or scored populations.
The adapter consumes retained baseline output and cannot become runtime mapping, decoding, classification, scoring, or report authority.
Core scanner timing remains unavailable until a real report-suppressed instrumentation seam exists.
Raw executable-return-byte presence remains unavailable for the external baselines because their native reports are decoded gadget outputs, not loader-authoritative byte scans.
